era: Gangsta Rap. E=7 -- FIRST GANGSTA RAP. Genre Stage 101. Tacoma. Nastymix Records. PNW gangsta rap, independent from LA. v1.49.323.

location.formative: Tacoma streets. Nastymix Records ecosystem. PNW hip-hop scene, early 1990s.

associated_musicians.name: Sir Mix-a-Lot
associated_musicians.relationship: Label head. Degree 179. Nastymix Records founder. Criminal Nation on his label.

associated_musicians.name: Kid Sensation
associated_musicians.relationship: Nastymix labelmate. Degree 177. Bass tradition. Same label infrastructure.

associated_musicians.name: High Performance
associated_musicians.relationship: Nastymix labelmate, Tacoma. Degree 178. Same city, same label, different genre.

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST GANGSTA RAP -- Genre Stage 101. Gangsta rap enters the engine. PNW emergence parallel to but independent from LA. Social conditions (deindustrialization, crack epidemic, policing) were not geographically limited.
examples: Criminal Nation: Tacoma gangsta rap on Nastymix.
N.W.A. *Straight Outta Compton* (1988): canonical LA origin.
PNW had its own gangsta rap, not a copy of LA.
Social conditions produced the genre independently.
theory_nodes.description: First gangsta rap. Genre Stage 101. Independent PNW emergence.

genealogy.relationship: Criminal Nation at 182. First gangsta rap in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Nastymix range (4 entries) -- four entries spanning bass, pop-crossover, and gangsta rap. The label was a platform for PNW hip-hop in all forms, not a monolithic sound.
theory_nodes.description: Nastymix Records: 4 entries, multiple genres. Platform, not monolith.

genealogy.relationship: Criminal Nation at 182 expands Nastymix range to gangsta rap.

theory_nodes.concept: Tacoma third entry -- Tacoma is not Seattle. Its own identity, streets, and relationship with systemic issues. Three entries (143, 178, 182) establish Tacoma as a distinct voice.
theory_nodes.description: Tacoma third entry. Distinct from Seattle. Own identity.

genealogy.relationship: Criminal Nation at 182. Third Tacoma entry. New genre.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 trio (181-183) -- three consecutive E=7 entries opening Quadrant III. Maximum energy concentration. The engine launches the second half at full power.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 trio. Second of three consecutive maximum energy entries.

genealogy.relationship: Criminal Nation at 182. Second of the E=7 trio.

cross_references.relationship: NASTYMIX! Criminal Nation on Mix-a-Lot's label. Same infrastructure, different genre. Label's range demonstrated.
NASTYMIX! Bass tradition. Same label. Criminal Nation shows Nastymix went beyond bass.
TACOMA + NASTYMIX! Same city, same label, different genre. Bass (178) vs gangsta (182).
E=7 STREAK! First of three consecutive E=7 entries.
E=7 STREAK! Third consecutive E=7. The trio completes at 183.
